title: 七面钟之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\144-七面钟之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Eileen Bondel visits the Chimney House to speak with the housekeeper, Mrs. Howell. During their conversation, Bondel observes that the household staff includes two inefficient country maids and a competent housemaid who is Howell's niece. Bondel notes that Howell appears to bully Lady Cuttel and observes that the servants do not seem to like Sir Osbert Cutt. When Bondel inquires about the death of Mr. Gerry Wade, Mrs. Howell provides no significant information.

Bondel then summons the butler, Tredwell, to ask about Alfred, a former employee who left for London a month prior. Tredwell introduces the new footman, John, noting he was previously employed by Lord Montfort. Upon returning to the Chimney House, Bondel observes that John has a military bearing and an unusual head shape. Reflecting on the name "Bauer," Bondel suspects John has German rather than Swiss ancestry and notes that his arrival coincided with the period leading up to Mr. Wade's death.

Bondel informs her father, Lord Kettlemere, that she intends to visit her aunt, Lady Marchioness Ketteman. Lord Kettlemere warns her against visiting the formidable widow, but Bondel also asks him about Sir Osbert Cutt’s profession; he explains that Sir Osbert is in the steel business and owns large factories. As she travels to London, Bondel attempts to connect the steel industry, infant welfare, the inventor Hel Eberhard, and the Secretary of State for Aviation, concluding that one of these interests must be a facade. Finally, Bondel meets with Lady Marchioness Ketteman, a large woman with a hooked nose and a beard, who expresses disapproval regarding the renting of the Chimney House.
